Title: User Login Success
Preconditions: User account exists and is active.
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to log in with valid credentials.
2. System verifies the provided credentials against the stored ones.
3. System returns true if the credentials match.
Expected Result: User successfully logs in and receives a confirmation message.

Title: User Login Failure - Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: User account exists but with different credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to log in with invalid credentials.
2. System verifies the provided credentials against the stored ones.
3. System returns false if the credentials do not match.
Expected Result: User fails to log in and receives an error message.

Title: User Logout
Test Steps:
1. User initiates the logout process.
2. System clears the session and logs out the user.
Expected Result: User is logged out and receives a confirmation message.

Title: Admin Login Success
Preconditions: Admin account exists and is active.
Test Steps:
1. Admin attempts to log in with valid credentials.
2. System verifies the provided credentials against the stored ones.
3. System returns true if the credentials match.
Expected Result: Admin successfully logs in and receives a confirmation message.

Title: Admin Login Failure - Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: Admin account exists but with different credentials.
Test Steps:
1. Admin attempts to log in with invalid credentials.
2. System verifies the provided credentials against the stored ones.
3. System returns false if the credentials do not match.
Expected Result: Admin fails to log in and receives an error message.

Title: Admin Logout
Test Steps:
1. Admin initiates the logout process.
2. System clears the session and logs out the admin.
Expected Result: Admin is logged out and receives a confirmation message.

Title: Add Product Success
Test Data:
- Product name: "New Product"
- Product description: "This is a new product."
Test Steps:
1. User/Admin adds a new product with valid details.
2. System validates the input and adds the product to the inventory.
Expected Result: Product is added successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Remove Product Success
Preconditions: User/Admin is logged in and product exists.
Test Steps:
1. User/Admin removes an existing product.
2. System validates the input and removes the product from the inventory.
Expected Result: Product is removed successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Remove Nonexistent Product
Test Steps:
1. User/Admin attempts to remove a nonexistent product.
2. System validates the input and returns an error message.
Expected Result: Error message indicating the product does not exist is displayed.

Title: Add Item to Cart Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and item exists.
Test Steps:
1. User adds an item to the cart.
2. System validates the input and adds the item to the cart.
Expected Result: Item is added successfully to the cart and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Remove Item from Cart Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and item exists in the cart.
Test Steps:
1. User removes an item from the cart.
2. System validates the input and removes the item from the cart.
Expected Result: Item is removed successfully from the cart and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Remove Nonexistent Item from Cart
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to remove a nonexistent item from the cart.
2. System validates the input and returns an error message.
Expected Result: Error message indicating the item does not exist in the cart is displayed.

Title: User Login via Firebase Authentication Success
Preconditions: User account exists and is active.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates login process.
2. System sends login request to FirebaseAuth.
3. FirebaseAuth verifies the credentials and returns the current user.
4. System retrieves the current user's details from FirebaseAuth.
Expected Result: User successfully logs in and receives a confirmation message.

Title: User Check Status via Firebase Authentication Success
Test Steps:
1. User initiates check status process.
2. System sends check status request to FirebaseAuth.
3. FirebaseAuth verifies the user status and returns the current user.
4. System retrieves the current user's details from FirebaseAuth.
Expected Result: User status is confirmed and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: User Create Account via Firebase Authentication Success
Test Steps:
1. User initiates account creation process.
2. System sends create account request to FirebaseAuth.
3. FirebaseAuth verifies the credentials and creates a new user.
4. System retrieves the newly created user's details from FirebaseAuth.
Expected Result: User account is created successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: User Interacts with Application Success
Test Steps:
1. User initiates interaction with the application.
2. Application processes the user's request.
3. Application interacts with GestoreCalendario.
4. Application interacts with Database.
Expected Result: User's request is processed successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: User Starts Calendar Success
Test Steps:
1. User initiates calendar start process.
2. System sends start request to Sistema.
3. Sistema manages the calendar start process.
4. Calendar is started successfully.
Expected Result: Calendar is started successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Add Menu to Restaurant Success
Preconditions: User/Admin is logged in and restaurant exists.
Test Steps:
1. User/Admin adds a new menu to the restaurant.
2. System validates the input and adds the menu to the restaurant.
Expected Result: Menu is added successfully to the restaurant and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Remove Menu from Restaurant Success
Preconditions: User/Admin is logged in and menu exists.
Test Steps:
1. User/Admin removes an existing menu from the restaurant.
2. System validates the input and removes the menu from the restaurant.
Expected Result: Menu is removed successfully from the restaurant and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Remove Nonexistent Menu from Restaurant
Test Steps:
1. User/Admin attempts to remove a nonexistent menu from the restaurant.

Title: Train Starts at Station Success
Test Steps:
1. Train starts at the specified station.
2. System updates the train's status to "started".
3. System sends a notification to the station.
Expected Result: Train starts successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Train Stops at Station Success
Test Steps:
1. Train stops at the specified station.
2. System updates the train's status to "stopped".
3. System sends a notification to the station.
Expected Result: Train stops successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Train Retrieves Schedule from Calendar Success
Test Steps:
1. Train retrieves its schedule from the specified calendar.
2. System fetches the train's schedule from the calendar.
3. System updates the train's schedule.
Expected Result: Train's schedule is retrieved successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Display Payload on Train Success
Test Steps:
1. Payload is displayed on the specified train.
2. System updates the train's payload display.
3. System sends a notification to the train.
Expected Result: Payload is displayed successfully on the train and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Connect to Database Success
Test Steps:
1. User initiates database connection process.
2. System sends connect request to Database.
3. Database establishes a connection.
Expected Result: Database connection is established successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Disconnect from Database Success
Preconditions: User is connected to the database.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates database disconnection process.
2. System sends disconnect request to Database.
3. Database closes the connection.
Expected Result: Database connection is closed successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Start Server Success
Test Steps:
1. User initiates server start process.
2. System sends start request to Server.
3. Server starts and initializes the database connection.
Expected Result: Server starts successfully and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Stop Server Success
Test Steps:
1. User initiates server stop process.
2. System sends stop request to Server.
3. Server stops and closes the database connection.
Expected Result: Server stops successfully
